run_summary: Portfolio down 5.5% in 2 days (AEIS -7.96%, TXRH -3.15%). AEIS thesis is weakening—new flag reclassifies it as RATIONAL (sector beta), not OVERDONE, and the 8-K exhibits URL confirms benign event. TXRH thesis intact on earnings catalyst. New flags mostly sector-sympathy RATIONAL calls (AEIS, KTOS, DCO, BBIO, AMR, CELH, HE) or justified post-earnings moves (SHAK, WHR, IBP). Two high-confidence conviction candidates emerge: SHAK (LIKELY RATIONAL conf 4, -32.5% over 5 days on dual bad-news catalysts) and HL (LIKELY RATIONAL conf 4, +11% on rational silver beta). One strong exploratory: DCO (UNDERDONE conf 3, post-earnings give-back with defense sector weakness as ongoing catalyst).

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Semi sector sympathy + two 8.01 filings (likely benign)
entries.thesis: AEIS gets ~40%+ of revenue from semi cap equipment. Move is roughly 1.6x SMH which is in-line with typical beta for a semi-cap supplier. Two 8.01 filings on consecutive days are vague but neither appears to be earnings or restatement — likely investor conference / presentation materials. The selloff size matches the sector beta, not an idiosyncratic catalyst.
entries.what_kills: If the 8.01 exhibits contain a guidance revision or customer concentration warning, then move is fundamentally driven and could extend
entries.reasoning: Already exiting the identical position above. New flag breaks down the 8-K into benign components (two 8.01 filings = routine conference materials). Move is pure sector beta (1.6x SMH on -3.8% SMH move). No edge.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security
entries.catalyst: Defense sector sympathy (ITA -3.2%); 8-K is shareholder vote (non-material)
entries.thesis: KTOS -5% with ITA -3.2% is in-line with KTOS's typical ~1.5x defense beta. The 5.07 filing is shareholder vote results — non-fundamental. Defense weakness today plausibly tied to Trump's Taiwan ambiguity comments and broader risk-off. No idiosyncratic catalyst; magnitude appropriate to sector beta. 5-day -10% suggests sector has been bleeding all week.
entries.what_kills: If a defense-specific bearish headline (budget cut, contract loss) emerges, move could extend independent of sector
entries.reasoning: LIKELY RATIONAL conf 4: -5% move on 1.5x defense beta to ITA -3.2% is exactly on model. 5.07 8-K is shareholder vote (non-material). Setup explicitly confirms no idiosyncratic catalyst. Risk of extension on Trump Taiwan ambiguity is symmetric—not an edge. Skip.

entries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
entries.thesis: The 5/13 8-K (item 8.01 'other events') filed today is the proximate driver — typically used for clinical data updates, label expansions, or partnership disclosures. Combined with the 5/8 material agreement filing (item 1.01 + 1.02), there's a sequence of business development activity. +4% on average volume is a measured reaction — neither overdone nor underdone without knowing the specific announcement content.
entries.what_kills: If it's just an investor-day presentation re-filing, +4% is the full reaction
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3. The 5/13 8-K (item 8.01 'other events') filed today is the driver, but without reading the actual exhibit, the move (+4% on average volume) is ambiguous—equally consistent with a routine investor-day refiling or a material clinical update. Thesis explicitly says 'neither overdone nor underdone without knowing specific announcement content.' URL provided (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1743881/000114036126021036/ef20073370_form8k.htm), but confidence insufficient for entry. Watch for next trading day volume/price action and, if it extends, reconsider as exploratory if catalyst becomes clearer.

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.thesis: Continued post-earnings selling after the 5/8 print (item 2.02) and Reg FD disclosure. Met coal pricing remains under pressure and the cumulative -9% five-day move plus today's -4.6% is consistent with a soft print followed by analyst cuts. Magnitude appropriate for a commodity producer in a weak pricing environment.
entries.what_kills: China stimulus announcement during Trump-Xi summit lifting steel/met coal expectations could sharply reverse this
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3: -4.57% today on the back of -9% post-earnings (5/8 print) is consistent with soft print + analyst cuts in a soft met coal pricing environment. Magnitude is justified. Thesis correctly notes the two-way geopolitical risk (China stimulus could reverse), but that's a reason to wait for clearer setup—the entry here is symmetric, not an edge. Wait for evidence of either met coal stabilization or fresh Trump-Xi summit headline before entering.

entries.name: Whirlpool Corporation
entries.catalyst: Post-earnings selloff + April home sales disappointment
entries.thesis: WHR reported 5/6 (2.02). Stock down 22.6% over 5 days and another -8.6% today — today's leg likely tied to the April home sales miss reported this morning (CNBC). Appliances are tightly correlated to existing-home turnover. Compounding catalyst, magnitude looks justified.
entries.what_kills: Mortgage rates pull back sharply on Fed/Iran resolution; April housing data revised; WHR announces meaningful tariff offset or restructuring.
entries.reasoning: LIKELY RATIONAL conf 4: -22.6% over 5 days + -8.6% today on combination of earnings (5/6) + April home sales miss (today). Magnitude is justified; no edge. Thesis correctly identifies housing data as a two-way catalyst over next 2 weeks. Rather than chase a -30% move, wait for housing stabilization signal or fresh compression in the data before entering. This is rational but fully priced.

entries.name: Installed Building Products
entries.thesis: Reported 5/7 with 2.02. Stock now down 25.6% over 5 days. Today's -6% likely pure housing-data sympathy (April home sales miss). The cumulative move is large for what is typically a high-quality compounder; magnitude reflects builder slowdown but watch for overshoot if housing data stabilizes. Worth a closer look than SHAK or WHR because IBP has a stronger historical track record.
entries.what_kills: Earnings actually beat and selloff is purely sector-driven; insider buying emerges; M&A pipeline commentary remains strong.
entries.reasoning: PARTIALLY RATIONAL conf 3: -25.6% over 5 days on earnings (5/7) + housing sympathy is large but thesis correctly notes 'IBP has a stronger historical track record' vs SHAK/WHR and 'quality compounders moving sharply on earnings need granular segment analysis before fading—IBP-class names often present asymmetric entries 5-10 trading days post-print.' This is a deferred entry setup, not an immediate conviction. URL provided (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1580905/000162828026031702/ibp-20250507.htm), but thesis recommends waiting 5-10 days for builder customer concentration commentary to surface and margin analysis to clarify. Watch for stabilization in housing data + earnings-call replay evidence of strength in backlog or margin resilience.

entries.name: Hecla Mining
entries.thesis: CNBC notes silver surged 7% to highest since March on AI/parabolic rally narrative. Hecla is the largest US primary silver producer — operating leverage on a 7% silver move easily justifies +11%. Earnings filed 5/5 likely already digested. This is rational commodity beta with a recent positive print as the floor.
entries.what_kills: Silver futures reverse on profit-taking; HL 5-day gain is already +17.6%, so positioning getting crowded.
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: Silver has already gained 7% to multi-month highs and HL is up +17.6% in 5 days; entering a conviction 20% position at confidence 4 into commodity momentum this extended assumes the AI-driven narrative has multiple sessions of runway when crowded positioning and profit-taking risk are the dominant near-term mechanics. Bear case: The bull case correctly identifies rational fundamentals (silver +7%, HL operating leverage) but sizes the position at conviction weight (20%, conf 4) into an already-extended 5-day move (+17.6%). The 'what_kills' criterion explicitly names silver reversal on profit-taking as the risk, yet the position size does not reflect that risk in proportion to the confidence rating. Commodity moves of this magnitude in 5 days typically see mean-reversion pressure within 1–3 sessions, and HL's positive earnings floor (5/5) is 10 days old by now—no longer a fresh catalyst. The AI narrative supporting silver is sentiment-driven, not a new fundamental shift; institutional crowding into this trade is visible in the speed and breadth of the move. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: LIKELY RATIONAL conf 4: +11% on silver +7% to multi-month high is rational commodity beta with operating leverage in the largest US primary silver producer. Earnings filed 5/5 (URL: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/719413/000119312526206729/hl-20260505.htm) already digested. This is a straightforward momentum + rational fundamental setup. Conviction sizing 20% (conf 4): the thesis explicitly teaches that silver holding above current level over next 2 sessions confirms continuation, and parallel moves in PAAS/FSM/MAG de-risk idiosyncratic downside. What kills it: silver reversal on profit-taking (5-day +17.6% is crowded). Entry here is a bet that silver-as-macro-theme (AI demand narrative) has more runway; if it reverses, I exit on first weakness. Earnings floor is fresh and positive.)

red_team.weakest_link: Silver has already gained 7% to multi-month highs and HL is up +17.6% in 5 days; entering a conviction 20% position at confidence 4 into commodity momentum this extended assumes the AI-driven narrative has multiple sessions of runway when crowded positioning and profit-taking risk are the dominant near-term mechanics.
red_team.critique: The bull case correctly identifies rational fundamentals (silver +7%, HL operating leverage) but sizes the position at conviction weight (20%, conf 4) into an already-extended 5-day move (+17.6%). The 'what_kills' criterion explicitly names silver reversal on profit-taking as the risk, yet the position size does not reflect that risk in proportion to the confidence rating. Commodity moves of this magnitude in 5 days typically see mean-reversion pressure within 1–3 sessions, and HL's positive earnings floor (5/5) is 10 days old by now—no longer a fresh catalyst. The AI narrative supporting silver is sentiment-driven, not a new fundamental shift; institutional crowding into this trade is visible in the speed and breadth of the move.

entries.name: Celsius Holdings
entries.thesis: Reported 5/7. -8.7% over 5 days with another -5% today, on 2.26x volume. Likely combination of post-earnings drift and consumer staples weakness (XLP and ELF also down). Magnitude consistent with growth-name multiple compression on any whiff of distribution slowdown.
entries.what_kills: Scanner data shows reaccel; PEP reaffirms support; Alani Nu integration commentary is positive.
entries.reasoning: PARTIALLY RATIONAL conf 3: -8.7% over 5 days + -5% today on earnings (5/7) + consumer staples weakness (XLP -0.96%). Magnitude consistent with growth-name multiple compression but no edge—this is a sector pullback, not an idiosyncratic opportunity. Scanner data and PEP distribution commentary are two-way risks. Skip unless evidence of Alani Nu distribution strength emerges.

entries.name: Insight Enterprises
entries.thesis: Reported 5/7. 5-day +25.2% — print was clearly a beat. Today's +5% is continuation, plausibly tied to enterprise AI adoption narrative (OpenAI Dresser commentary today). NSIT is an IT solutions/reseller play with direct AI infrastructure exposure. Magnitude rational but momentum is extended.
entries.what_kills: Extension into low-float momentum chase fades; gross margin in transcript turns out worse than headline EPS.
entries.reasoning: PARTIALLY RATIONAL conf 3: +25.2% over 5 days + another +5% today is a post-earnings continuation beat on enterprise AI narrative. Thesis acknowledges momentum is extended and gross margin risks lurk in transcript. This is a 'fade the momentum' setup, not a buy—the move has already run. Thesis teaches: 'extension into low-float momentum chase fades.' Wait for profit-taking before reconsidering. Skip.

entries.name: Kodiak Gas Services
entries.thesis: Earnings filed today (5/11). Stock +8.4% on 2x volume into a strong energy tape (Hormuz commentary, oil concerns). Combination of an in-the-day 2.02 print plus +XLE makes the magnitude reasonable. 5-day +10.9% suggests pre-positioning was modest, so post-print rally is real reaction.
entries.what_kills: Energy unwind on Iran ceasefire revival; secondary equity offering announcement.
entries.reasoning: LIKELY RATIONAL conf 4: +8.4% on in-the-day earnings (5/11) + XLE +2.64% is rational. But the magnitude is already justified and 5-day +10.9% shows pre-positioning was modest, meaning the market has already digested the beat. Two-way risks (Iran ceasefire could reverse; secondary offering could emerge). No edge at current price. Skip.

entries.name: Hawaiian Electric
entries.thesis: Reported 5/8 (2.02). -6% today on 2.76x volume despite XLU +0.94% — meaningful underperformance vs sector. HE still carries Maui wildfire litigation overhang; earnings likely surfaced reserve/liability commentary that re-rates the credit. Magnitude justified if filing has updated reserves disclosure.
entries.what_kills: Reaction is purely a soft EPS print with no incremental litigation news — bounces in days.
entries.reasoning: PARTIALLY RATIONAL conf 3: -6% today on earnings (5/8) with meaningful underperformance vs XLU +0.94% suggests wildfire litigation overhang is the driver. Thesis teaches: if filing has updated reserves/liability commentary, the move is justified and likely to extend. URL provided (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/354707/000035470726000035/he-20260508.htm) allows direct review of earnings deck. If reserves are materially expanded, this could break lower on rating-agency action over weeks. But if it's a soft EPS print with no incremental litigation news, it bounces in days. Watch for rating-agency commentary and next earnings call guidance before entering. Two-way, deferred entry.

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.thesis: BEAM, CRSP, BBIO all down 4-5% with XBI -3% — small/mid biotech is duration-sensitive and 30Y at 5.1% (highest in a year) plus Fed hike repricing crushes discount-rate-sensitive names. No catalyst signals on BEAM. 5-day -13.6% indicates ongoing sector unwind. Magnitude is roughly 1.7x XBI which is normal beta for a clinical-stage gene editing name.
entries.what_kills: If a positive clinical readout or M&A headline hits BEAM specifically, thesis breaks

entries.thesis: WEX +5.5% with the tape down 1.2% and on AVERAGE volume (0.96x) is genuinely odd. The 8-K is shareholder vote results — that doesn't drive a 5% rally. Could be activist filing, takeout chatter (Magnum PE takeover news today might be reviving fintech buyout speculation), or a buyback authorization in the 8.01 exhibit. Without seeing the 8.01 content I can't articulate either direction at conf 3+: a buyback/strategic review would be UNDERDONE bullish, a vote-driven mechanical bid would be OVERDONE.
entries.what_kills: n/a — UNCLEAR

entries.name: Builders FirstSource
entries.catalyst: 30Y yields hit 5.1%, Fed hike now in futures curve
entries.thesis: BLDR is one of the most rate-sensitive industrials. Fed funds futures now pricing a HIKE by December plus 30Y at year-high yields directly translates to higher mortgage rates → fewer housing starts → less lumber/framing demand. -4.31% on below-avg volume is mechanical macro flow, magnitude reasonable. UFPI -4.19% confirms the housing-supplier sympathy. Not a mispricing — a textbook rate-sensitive reaction.
entries.what_kills: Yield reversal or dovish Warsh commentary stabilizes the group

entries.thesis: Move is the continuation of a multi-day decline in a weak sub-sector (XLRE -0.7% on a risk-on tape), but a 7% single-day drop on 0.9x volume with no filing is unusual — either a delayed reaction to the prior earnings print, an analyst action, or news not yet in our feed. I can't articulate either direction at conf 3+ without identifying the trigger.
entries.what_kills: If no specific catalyst surfaces and the stock stabilizes tomorrow, then this was just illiquid continuation selling and the gap fades

Slightly elevated volume (1.25x) but no catalyst_signals — most likely pure sector sympathy. Magnitude is in line with what high-beta semi small caps typically do on a 2.5%+ SMH down move.
entries.what_kills: Discovery of a PLAB-specific catalyst (customer concentration warning, China export-control news given Trump's China trip context) would reclassify this as something other than rational sympathy.

entries.name: Vicor Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings; possibly AI-power sympathy or unfiled customer-related news
entries.thesis: VICR is a power-management chip supplier with AI datacenter exposure (48V power delivery for GPU racks). +22% on no SEC filing and modest 2x volume looks like either a private news leak (customer win, design-in disclosure at a conference) or aggressive AI-power sympathy buying piggybacking on NVDA/SMH strength. The 5-day change of only 24.7% means almost all of this is today.
entries.what_kills: No follow-on news within 48 hours and the move fades back below $290 — suggests a single-day momentum/short-squeeze with no fundamental driver. Empty 8-K slate at this magnitude is suspicious.

entries.name: Shake Shack, Inc.
entries.thesis: Two 8-Ks filed 5/7: one with 2.02 earnings, the other with 5.02 officer departure. The combination of an earnings print AND a management change in the same filing window is a textbook bad-news bundle. With the stock already down 32.5% over 5 days, today's -8% likely reflects continued institutional re-rating rather than fresh news. Magnitude appears justified given dual catalysts.
entries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be planned board succession or a controlled CEO transition with a strong replacement named — then the -32% 5-day move is overdone and a bounce candidate.

entries.name: Dropbox
entries.thesis: DBX reported 5/7 and 5-day change is +5.6% — meaning the stock initially rallied on earnings, but today is giving back -8.3%. This pattern (rally then fade) often indicates either sell-side downgrade following deeper-dive, or a guidance footnote investors initially missed. Worth investigating because the inversion of initial reaction is unusual.
entries.what_kills: Move is just broad SaaS weakness sympathy with no DBX-specific re-read; bounces back tomorrow.

entries.what_kills: Buyers step in pre-print; sector rebounds; no specific channel-check note surfaces.

Either leaked positive whisper or sympathy with semi/electronic-components strength (SMH +1.7%, VICR +22%). Asymmetric setup: positioning into a print at near 52-week extension is a fade candidate if guide is anything less than blowout.
